artPrompt: A grim midnight reservoir under rain, a dredging crane silhouetted against ink-black sky, ropes hauling vague heavy shapes from black water lit by a single amber lamp on the dock, ripples bleeding outward in smoky-brown wash. The lower frame dissolves into oily darkness, the dredged secrets only suggested. A dark deed by the water, rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: The Whateverville Reservoir Job
text:
Each opponent sacrifices a creature. For each creature sacrificed this way, you create a Treasure and a Favor token. They went down to the reservoir. They did not all come back up.
Whatever's living in that reservoir owes the Don too, by the way. He has a marker on it. He has a marker on everything.
flavorText: The dredging crew found three cars, a piano, and a number of goblins who'd missed payments.
